text_en: Reaffirming the commitments to gender equality and the advancement of women made at the Millennium Summit 3 , the 2005 World Summit 4 and other major United Nations summits, conferences and special sessions, and reaffirming also that their full, effective and accelerated implementation is essential to achieving the internationally agreed development goals, including the Millennium Development Goals,

text_en: Welcoming the progress made towards gender equality, but stressing that challenges and obstacles continue to hamper the implementation of the Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action and the outcome of the twenty-third special session,

text_en: Reaffirming the important role of women in conflict prevention, resolution and peacebuilding,

items_en: 3. Recognizes that the implementation of the Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action and the implementation of the obligations of States parties to the Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination against Women 11 are mutually reinforcing for the achievement of gender equality and the empowerment of women, welcomes in this regard the contribution of the Committee on the Elimination of Discrimination against Women to the promotion of the implementation of the Platform for Action and the outcome of the twenty-third special session, and invites States parties to the Convention to include in their reports to the Committee under article 18 of the Convention information on their measures to improve the implementation of the Platform for Action at the national level;

4. Calls upon Governments, the United Nations system, other international and regional organizations, all sectors of civil society, including non-governmental organizations, and all men and women, to commit themselves fully to promoting the implementation of the Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action and the outcome of the twenty-third special session of the General Assembly and to intensify efforts to that end;

5. Calls upon States parties to comply fully with their obligations under the Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination against Women and its Optional Protocol 12 and to take into account the concluding observations and general recommendations of the Committee, urges them to consider limiting the scope of their possible reservations to the Convention, to formulate these reservations as precisely and narrowly as possible and to review them regularly with a view to withdrawing them, so as to ensure that no reservation is incompatible with the object and purpose of the Convention, and also urges all Member States that have not yet done so to consider ratifying or acceding to the Convention and to consider signing or ratifying or acceding to the Optional Protocol;

6. Encourages all actors, including Governments, the United Nations system, other international organizations and civil society, to continue to assist the Commission on the Status of Women in playing its central role in the follow-up to and review of the implementation of the Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action and the outcome of the twenty-third special session, and, where appropriate, in the implementation of its recommendations, and welcomes in this regard the revised programme and methods of work adopted by the Commission at its fiftieth session, 13 which pay particular attention to the exchange of practical information, lessons learned and good practices as a means of overcoming obstacles to the full implementation of the Platform for Action,

items_en: 7. Calls upon Governments, relevant United Nations funds, programmes, bodies and specialized agencies, within their respective mandates, as well as international financial institutions and all relevant entities of civil society, including non-governmental organizations, to intensify their efforts to ensure the full and effective implementation of the Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action and the outcome of the twenty-third special session, inter alia, to this end:

(a) To demonstrate their political will and sustained commitment to take further action at the national, regional and international levels, including the systematic adoption of a gender perspective, which requires, inter alia, the development and use of gender equality indicators, where appropriate, in all policies and programmes, the promotion of the empowerment of women and their full and equal participation and the strengthening of international cooperation;

(b) To ensure the promotion, protection, respect and full enjoyment of the human rights and fundamental freedoms of women and girls, including through strict compliance by States with their obligations under all human rights instruments, in particular the Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination against Women;

(c) Ensure the full and equal representation of women and their full participation in political, social and economic decision-making, which is essential for gender equality, as well as the empowerment of women and girls, a key factor in the fight against poverty;

(d) Actively involve women at all levels of environmental decision-making, integrate a gender perspective into sustainable development policies and programmes, and establish or strengthen national, regional and international mechanisms to assess the impact of development and environmental policies on women;

(e) Provide technical assistance to women, in particular in developing countries, to promote human resources development, environmentally sound technologies and women's entrepreneurship on an ongoing basis;

(f) To uphold the rule of law, including laws, and to continue efforts to repeal laws, policies and practices that discriminate against women and girls, adopt new laws and promote practices that protect their rights;

(g) Strengthen the role of national institutional mechanisms for gender equality and the advancement of women, including through appropriate financial and other assistance, in order to increase their direct impact on women;
(h) To implement socio-economic policies that promote sustainable development and include poverty reduction programmes,

in particular for women and girls, to strengthen the provision of effective, affordable and accessible public and social services, including education and training at all levels, as well as permanent and sustainable social protection and security systems for women throughout their lives, to ensure equal access to such services and schemes and to support national efforts in these areas;

( i) Take further measures to ensure that the education system and the media, within the limits permitted by freedom of expression, encourage the use of non-stereotypical, balanced and diverse images of women, presenting them as essential agents of development and promoting non-discriminatory roles for women and men in private and public life;

(j) To integrate a gender and human rights perspective into health-related research policies, programmes and activities, to pay attention to the special needs and priorities of women and girls, to ensure women's right to the enjoyment of the highest attainable standard of health and access to adequate and affordable health care, including sexual, reproductive, maternal and essential obstetric care, in accordance with the Programme of Action of the International Conference on Population and Development 14 , and to recognize that women, deprived of economic autonomy and independence, have become more vulnerable to all kinds of risks, including HIV/AIDS, malaria, tuberculosis and other poverty-related diseases;

(k) To eliminate gender inequalities and gender-based abuse and violence; to help women and adolescent girls to better protect themselves from the risk of HIV infection, primarily by providing them with health care and services, in particular sexual and reproductive health, and by giving them full access to all the information and education they need; to ensure that women can exercise their right to control their sexuality and to take decisions freely and responsibly in this area, in order to better protect themselves from the risk of HIV infection, including sexual and reproductive health, without being subjected to coercion, discrimination and violence; and to take all necessary measures to create an enabling environment for the empowerment of women and increase their economic independence, reaffirming in this regard the importance of the role of men and boys in achieving gender equality;

(l) Strengthen national health and social infrastructure so as to strengthen measures to promote women's access to public health services and take measures at the national level to address human resource shortages in health, including by developing, financing and implementing, within the framework of national development strategies, policies to improve training and management and to ensure effective recruitment, retention and allocation of health services;

text_en: health personnel, including through international cooperation in this field;

n ) De multiplier les partenariats entre les gouvernements, la sociÃĐtÃĐ civile et le secteur privÃĐ ;

p ) D'ÃĐliminer  les  barriÃĻres  structurelles  et  juridiques  ainsi  que  les stÃĐrÃĐotypes faisant obstacle Ã  l'ÃĐgalitÃĐ des sexes dans l'emploi, de promouvoir le principe ÂŦ Ã  travail ÃĐgal, salaire ÃĐgal Âŧ, de faire en sorte que la valeur du travail non rÃĐmunÃĐrÃĐ des femmes soit reconnue et d'ÃĐlaborer et promouvoir des politiques qui permettent de concilier l'emploi et les responsabilitÃĐs familiales ;

8. RÃĐaffirme que les Ãtats sont tenus d'agir avec la diligence voulue pour prÃĐvenir la violence Ã  l'ÃĐgard des femmes et des filles, offrir une protection Ã  celles qui en sont victimes, mener des enquÃŠtes sur ces actes et poursuivre et sanctionner leurs auteurs, et que tout manquement Ã  cette obligation porte atteinte aux droits et aux  libertÃĐs  fondamentaux  des  femmes  et  des  filles  en  mÃŠme  temps  qu'il  en empÃŠche  partiellement  ou  totalement  l'exercice,  et  demande  aux  gouvernements d'ÃĐlaborer et d'appliquer des lois et des stratÃĐgies propres Ã  ÃĐliminer la violence Ã  l'ÃĐgard des femmes et des filles ;

9. Encourage  vivement les gouvernements  Ã   continuer  d'encourager l'intervention et la contribution de la sociÃĐtÃĐ civile, en particulier des organisations non gouvernementales et des organisations de femmes, en faveur de l'application de la DÃĐclaration et du Programme d'action de Beijing ainsi que des textes issus de la vingt-troisiÃĻme session extraordinaire ;

10. DÃĐcide d'intensifier l'action menÃĐe par ses grandes commissions et ses organes subsidiaires pour intÃĐgrer pleinement une dÃĐmarche soucieuse de l'ÃĐgalitÃĐ des sexes dans leurs travaux, notamment en prÃŠtant une plus grande attention aux questions liÃĐes Ã  la condition de la femme soumises Ã  leur examen et relevant de leur  mandat,  ainsi  que  dans  les  travaux  de  toutes  les  rÃĐunions  au  sommet, confÃĐrences et sessions extraordinaires de l'Organisation des Nations Unies et dans leur suivi ;

11. Demande que les rapports ÃĐtablis par le SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral Ã  son intention et  Ã   celle  de  ses  organes  subsidiaires  rendent  systÃĐmatiquement  compte  des questions  relatives  Ã   l'ÃĐgalitÃĐ  des  sexes  au  moyen  d'analyses  qualitatives  et, lorsqu'elles  sont  disponibles,  de  donnÃĐes  quantitatives,  en  particulier  dans  des conclusions  et  recommandations  concrÃĻtes  prÃĐconisant  l'adoption  de  nouvelles mesures en faveur de l'ÃĐgalitÃĐ des sexes et de la promotion de la femme, le but ÃĐtant de faciliter l'ÃĐlaboration de politiques tenant compte des sexospÃĐcificitÃĐs ;

12. Engage  instamment les  gouvernements  et  toutes  les  composantes  des Nations Unies, y compris les organismes, fonds et programmes, et toutes les entitÃĐs intÃĐressÃĐes de la sociÃĐtÃĐ civile, Ã  intÃĐgrer une dÃĐmarche soucieuse de l'ÃĐgalitÃĐ des
items_en: (m) To mobilize adequate resources at the national and international levels, as well as new and additional resources for developing countries, in particular the least developed countries and countries with economies in transition, using all available funding mechanisms, including multilateral, bilateral and private sources of financing;
( n ) Increase partnerships between governments, civil society and the private sector;

o ) To encourage the sharing of responsibilities between men and boys and women and girls in promoting gender equality, which is absolutely essential to achieving the goals of gender equality, development and peace;

( p ) To eliminate structural and legal barriers and stereotypes that impede gender equality in employment, to promote the principle of equal pay for equal work, to ensure that the value of women's unpaid work is recognized and to develop and promote policies that reconcile employment and family responsibilities ;

8. Reaffirms the obligation of States to act with due diligence to prevent, protect, investigate, prosecute and punish violence against women and girls, and that failure to do so violates the human rights and freedoms of women and girls at the same time as it partially or totally impedes the exercise of those rights and freedoms, and calls upon Governments to develop and implement laws and strategies to eliminate violence against women and girls;

9. Strongly encourages Governments to continue to encourage the involvement and contribution of civil society, in particular non-governmental organizations and women's organizations, in the implementation of the Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action and the outcome of the twenty-third special session;

10. Decides to intensify the efforts of its Main Committees and subsidiary bodies to fully integrate a gender perspective into their work, including by paying greater attention to issues related to the status of women under their consideration and within their mandates, as well as to the work of all United Nations summits, conferences and special sessions and their follow-up;

11. Requests that the reports of the Secretary-General to the General Assembly and its subsidiary bodies systematically reflect gender equality issues through qualitative analysis and, where available, quantitative data, in particular in concrete conclusions and recommendations for further action on gender equality and the advancement of women, with a view to facilitating the development of gender-sensitive policies;

12. Urges Governments and all components of the United Nations system, including agencies, funds and programmes, and all relevant entities of civil society, to integrate a gender perspective into their work.

text_en: in the implementation of and follow-up to all United Nations summits, conferences and special sessions, including the high-level commemorative plenary segment on follow-up to the outcome of the special session of the General Assembly on children in 2007, the thirteenth session of the Conference of the Parties to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change and the third session of the Conference of the Parties serving as the meeting of the Parties to the Kyoto Protocol, Bali, Indonesia, in 2007, the Follow-up International Conference on Financing for Development to Review the Implementation of the Monterrey Consensus, Doha, in 2008, and the Third High-level Forum on Aid Effectiveness, Accra, in 2008;

17. RÃĐaffirme le  rÃīle  primordial  et  essentiel  qu'elle-mÃŠme  et  le  Conseil ÃĐconomique et social ont Ã  jouer et le rÃīle central de la Commission de la condition de la femme dans la promotion de la femme et de l'ÃĐgalitÃĐ des sexes ;

items_en: 13. Reaffirms its call for the mainstreaming of a gender perspective in the consideration of all issues on the agenda of the Peacebuilding Commission and the Human Rights Council and in all their activities;

14. Encourages the Economic and Social Council to continue to ensure that gender mainstreaming is an integral part of its work and that of its subsidiary bodies, including through the implementation of its agreed conclusions 1997/2 of 18 July 1997 15 and its resolution 2004/4 of 7 July 2004;

15. Welcomes the ministerial declaration adopted at the high-level segment of the substantive session of 2007 of the Economic and Social Council, 16 in which, inter alia, it is reaffirmed that gender equality and the promotion and protection of the full enjoyment of all human rights and fundamental freedoms for all are essential for the eradication of poverty and hunger and that all countries should promote gender equality and the empowerment of women and, as called for, inter alia, in the Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action and in the outcome document of the twenty-third special session, identify and intensify measures to that end;

16. Calls upon all bodies responsible for programme and budgetary matters, including the Committee for Programme and Coordination, to ensure that programmes, plans and budgets clearly integrate a gender perspective;

17. Reaffirms the primary and essential role of the General Assembly and the Economic and Social Council and the central role of the Commission on the Status of Women in the advancement of women and gender equality;

18. Requests the Economic and Social Council to continue to encourage its functional commissions to mainstream a gender perspective in their follow-up to major United Nations conferences and summits and to identify more effective ways to ensure the implementation of the outcome documents on gender equality at the national level;
19. Stresses the catalytic role of the Commission on the Status of Women and the important contribution of the Economic and Social Council

23. Invite toutes  les  entitÃĐs  des  Nations  Unies  Ã   continuer  d'assurer activement l'application intÃĐgrale, effective et accÃĐlÃĐrÃĐe du Programme d'action de Beijing  et  des  textes  issus  de  la  vingt-troisiÃĻme  session  extraordinaire,  en s'appuyant notamment sur les travaux du Bureau de la ConseillÃĻre spÃĐciale pour la paritÃĐ des sexes et la promotion de la femme et sur ceux de la Division de la promotion de la femme, en maintenant des spÃĐcialistes des questions d'ÃĐgalitÃĐ des sexes et en veillant Ã  ce que tous les membres de leur personnel, en particulier ceux qui se trouvent sur le terrain, bÃĐnÃĐficient d'une formation et d'un suivi appropriÃĐ, y compris  des  outils,  des  directives  et  de  l'appui  nÃĐcessaires  pour  accÃĐlÃĐrer l'intÃĐgration d'une dÃĐmarche soucieuse de l'ÃĐgalitÃĐ des sexes dans leurs activitÃĐs, et rÃĐaffirme la nÃĐcessitÃĐ de renforcer les capacitÃĐs du systÃĻme des Nations Unies dans ce domaine ;

24. Prie le  SecrÃĐtaire  gÃĐnÃĐral  d'examiner  les  progrÃĻs  accomplis  et  de redoubler d'efforts pour avancer vers la rÃĐalisation de l'objectif de la paritÃĐ des sexes Ã  tous les niveaux du SecrÃĐtariat et dans l'ensemble du systÃĻme des Nations Unies, dans le strict respect du principe d'une rÃĐpartition gÃĐographique ÃĐquitable et conformÃĐment au paragraphe 3 de l'Article 101 de la Charte des Nations Unies, en tenant compte en particulier des femmes des pays en dÃĐveloppement et des pays les moins avancÃĐs, des pays en transition et des Ãtats Membres non reprÃĐsentÃĐs ou largement sous-reprÃĐsentÃĐs, et de veiller Ã  ce que le personnel d'encadrement et les dÃĐpartements  rendent  des  comptes  concernant  la  rÃĐalisation  de  la  paritÃĐ,  et encourage vivement les Ãtats Membres Ã  rechercher et Ã  prÃĐsenter rÃĐguliÃĻrement un plus grand nombre de candidates aux postes Ã  pourvoir dans le systÃĻme des Nations Unies, en particulier aux postes de haut niveau et aux postes de direction ;

items_en: and by the General Assembly in promoting and monitoring gender mainstreaming in the United Nations system;
20. Requests United Nations entities to mainstream the results of the work of the Commission on the Status of Women into their activities, within their mandates;

21. Reaffirms the commitment made at the 2005 World Summit to ensure the full and effective implementation of Security Council resolution 1325 (2000) of 31 October 2000, noting that seven years have elapsed since the adoption of that resolution and that the Council has held open debates on women and peace and security;

22. Urges Governments and the United Nations system to take further measures to ensure gender mainstreaming and the full and equal participation of women in all efforts to promote peace and security, including peace negotiations, peacekeeping and peacebuilding and post-conflict resolution, and to enhance the role of women in decision-making at all levels, including through the development of national strategies and action plans;

23. Invites all United Nations entities to continue to actively ensure the full, effective and accelerated implementation of the Beijing Platform for Action and the outcome of the twenty-third special session, building, inter alia, on the work of the Office of the Special Adviser on Gender Issues and Advancement of Women and the Division for the Advancement of Women, maintaining gender specialists and ensuring that all their staff, in particular those in the field, receive appropriate training and follow-up, including tools, guidelines and support to accelerate gender mainstreaming in their work, and reaffirms the need to strengthen the capacity of the United Nations system in this area;

24. Requests the Secretary-General to review the progress made and to intensify efforts towards achieving the goal of gender parity at all levels of the Secretariat and throughout the United Nations system, with full respect for the principle of equitable geographical distribution and in accordance with Article 101, paragraph 3, of the Charter of the United Nations, taking into account, in particular, women from developing and least developed countries, countries with economies in transition and Member States that are not represented or largely underrepresented, and to ensure that senior management and departments are accountable for achieving gender parity, and strongly encourages Member States to regularly seek and present more women candidates for posts in the United Nations system, in particular at the senior and senior levels;

25. Encourages its subsidiary bodies to mainstream gender issues in their discussions and outcome documents, including through the use of analyses, data and recommendations contained in the reports of the Secretary-General, and to follow up on outcome documents;
26. Requests that the reports submitted by the Secretary-General facilitate the development of gender-sensitive policies by including more

text_fr: systÃĐmatiquement des analyses, donnÃĐes et recommandations qualitatives sur ces questions ;
text_en: systematic qualitative analysis, data and recommendations on these issues;

items_fr: Prie les  organismes  des  Nations  Unies  de  continuer  Ã   s'efforcer d'atteindre  l'objectif  de  la  paritÃĐ  des  sexes,  notamment  avec  l'appui  actif  des responsables  de  la  coordination  des  questions  concernant  la  paritÃĐ,  et  prie  le SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral de prÃĐsenter un rapport oral Ã  la Commission de la condition de la femme  Ã   sa  cinquante-deuxiÃĻme  session  et  de  lui  faire  rapport  Ã   sa  soixantetroisiÃĻme session, dans le cadre de la question intitulÃĐe ÂŦ Promotion de la femme Âŧ, et de faire figurer dans son rapport sur la gestion des ressources humaines des informations  sur  la  situation  des  femmes  dans  le  systÃĻme  des  Nations  Unies, notamment sur les progrÃĻs accomplis et les obstacles rencontrÃĐs dans la rÃĐalisation de la paritÃĐ des sexes, des recommandations sur les moyens d'accÃĐlÃĐrer les progrÃĻs dans ce domaine, des statistiques Ã  jour, notamment sur le nombre, le pourcentage, les  fonctions  et  la  nationalitÃĐ  des  femmes  dans  l'ensemble  du  systÃĻme,  et  des informations sur les responsabilitÃĐs et les obligations redditionnelles incombant au Bureau de la gestion des ressources humaines du SecrÃĐtariat et au secrÃĐtariat du Conseil  des  chefs  de  secrÃĐtariat  des  organismes  des  Nations  Unies  pour  la coordination en ce qui concerne la promotion de la paritÃĐ des sexes ;

Prie le SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral de continuer Ã  lui rendre compte chaque annÃĐe, au  titre  de  la  question  intitulÃĐe  ÂŦ Promotion  de  la  femme Âŧ,  ainsi  qu'Ã   la Commission de la condition de la femme et au Conseil ÃĐconomique et social, du suivi et de l'ÃĐtat d'avancement de l'application de la DÃĐclaration et du Programme d'action de Beijing et des textes issus de la vingt-troisiÃĻme session extraordinaire, d'ÃĐvaluer  les  progrÃĻs  rÃĐalisÃĐs  dans  l'intÃĐgration  du  principe  de  la  paritÃĐ,  en indiquant  notamment  les  rÃĐalisations  marquantes,  les  enseignements  tirÃĐs  de l'expÃĐrience  et  les  bonnes  pratiques,  et  de  recommander  de  nouvelles  mesures propres Ã  renforcer l'application des textes prÃĐcitÃĐs.
items_en: Requests the United Nations system to continue its efforts to achieve the goal of gender balance, including with the active support of gender focal points, and requests the Secretary-General to submit an oral report to the Commission on the Status of Women at its fifty-second session and to report to the General Assembly at its sixty-third session, under the item entitled "Advancement of women", and to include in his report on human resources management information on the situation of women in the United Nations system, including on progress made and obstacles encountered in achieving gender balance, recommendations on how to accelerate progress in this area, updated statistics, including on the number, percentage, functions and nationality of women throughout the system, and information on the responsibilities and accountability of the Office of Human Resources Management of the Secretariat and the secretariat of the United Nations System Chief Executives Board for Coordination in the promotion of gender balance;

Requests the Secretary-General to continue to report annually to the General Assembly, under the item entitled "Advancement of women", as well as to the Commission on the Status of Women and the Economic and Social Council, on the follow-up to and progress made in the implementation of the Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action and the outcome of the twenty-third special session, to assess progress made in mainstreaming the gender perspective, including key achievements, lessons learned and good practices, and to recommend further measures to strengthen the implementation of the above-mentioned outcomes.

text_en: 76th plenary meeting 18 December 2007
